title: viola biflora subsp. carlottae
intro: Viola biflora subsp. carlottae, also known as Alpine Viola or Carlotta's Viola, is a subspecies of the Viola biflora plant, a flowering perennial plant native to the high-altitude regions of the European Alps. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral activities, attributed to the presence of flavonoids, glycosides, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Viola biflora subsp. carlottae include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a promising natural remedy for various health issues. Herbal preparations of Viola biflora subsp. carlottae can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to leverage its medicinal properties and health benefits.
